entry.title: Re: Error in Data Action after activate Data Access Contr...
entry.content: Seems your DAC is added to version dimension and user who trigger this data action has no write access to Actual version. You should either grant user with write access to Actual version or just remove DAC on version dimension.

entry.title: Re: Sending data in excel file from SAP to a shared folder of Virtual machine
entry.content:
I have tried to send using http_client interface methods and getting '405' http status code and method not allowed in response. Below is my code. let me know anything I can work on.

DATA: lt_binary_data TYPE TABLE OF x255,
lv_string TYPE string,
lv_len TYPE i.
DATA: lv_sjson TYPE string.
DATA: lr_data TYPE REF TO data.

CALL FUNCTION 'SCMS_XSTRING_TO_BINARY'
EXPORTING
buffer = lv_content
IMPORTING
output_length = lv_length
TABLES
binary_tab = lt_binary_tab.

DATA(lv_json) = /ui2/cl_json=>serialize( data = lv_content
pretty_name = abap_true ).


DATA(rfc_mulesoft_dest) = 'RFC_Name'.
DATA : class_http_client TYPE REF TO if_http_client.

cl_http_client=>create_by_destination(
EXPORTING
destination = rfc_mulesoft_dest
IMPORTING
client = class_http_client
EXCEPTIONS
argument_not_found = 1
destination_not_found = 2
destination_no_authority = 3
plugin_not_active = 4
internal_error = 5
OTHERS = 6 ) .
IF sy-subrc <> 0.
ENDIF.
* Set HTTP request method and headers
DATA(value) = 'VMFolderName/' && lv_filename.
class_http_client->request->set_method( 'POST' ).
class_http_client->request->set_header_field( name = 'Content-Type' value = 'application/octet-stream' ).
class_http_client->request->set_header_field( name = 'File-Path' value = value ).

lv_sjson = lv_json.
class_http_client->request->set_cdata( lv_sjson ).

*--> Send the request
CALL METHOD class_http_client->send
EXPORTING
timeout = if_http_client=>co_timeout_infinite
EXCEPTIONS
http_communication_failure = 1
http_invalid_state = 2
http_processing_failed = 3
http_invalid_timeout = 4
OTHERS = 5.
IF sy-subrc <> 0.
ENDIF.

*--> Get the response back
CALL METHOD class_http_client->receive
EXCEPTIONS
http_communication_failure = 1
http_invalid_state = 2
http_processing_failed = 3
OTHERS = 4.
IF sy-subrc NE 0.
http_status = class_http_client->response->get_header_field( '~status_code' ).
http_response = class_http_client->response->get_header_field( '~status_reason' ).
ENDIF.

entry.title: Can Host to Host bank integration be done using F-53?
entry.content:
Dear esteemed members,

My client is currently using F110 for making payments and Host to Host bank can be done using this t-code. Now, when processing payments using F110 it does not have a "Profit Center" field. So, we are exploring the T-code F-53 in which "Profit Center" is available.

Now, the real problem I am facing with is, can Host to Host bank integration be done if F-53 is used? If so, kindly guide me. Or please give me a high level overview of the same.

As always, thank you very much for all the help and guidance!

entry.content:
Hi,

we are using EWM and have serial numbers at bin level. We are not using Handling units. We do have the Serial number profile set for these materials

- On the WM Execution view, to have it active in EWm
- On the Work scheduling view, to have it active in IM.

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to include configurations done in a Fi...
entry.content:
Hi papa261190,

Is it possible to include configurations done in a Fiori app for aATP or records created in a TR?

-> Unfortunately not, some objects (e.g. BOP variants) require the generation of some coding in your system or the system generates GUIDs & dependent further table entries for each new CVC. Just transporting these objects does not generate code or generates the required GUIDs / objects.

For a high number of records created for product substitution you can e.g. download/upload them via excel and report "OVD_CVC_MASS_MAINT" or use an API to create these entries.

best regards,

Michael

entry.title: Re: SAP MM module
entry.content: 在 SAP MM 模块执行 303 工厂间转移时系统提示过账分录不存在，可能由多种原因导致，以下为你详细分析并给出对应的解决办法： ### 原因分析 1. **科目设置问题**：在 SAP 系统中，工厂间转移涉及到会计科目的记账。如果相关的自动过账配置没有正确设置，系统就无法确定对应的过账分录，从而提示过账分录不存在。例如，移动类型 303 对应的总账科目没有在 OBYC 事务码中正确维护。 2. **评估类设置问题**：评估类用于确定物料移动时的过账科目。如果物料主数据中的评估类设置不正确，或者评估类与自动过账配置不匹配，系统就无法找到合适的过账分录。 3. **移动类型配置问题**：移动类型 303 的配置可能存在错误或不完整。例如，移动类型的记账规则、评估类分配等设置不正确，会导致系统无法生成正确的过账分录。 4. **物料主数据问题**：物料主数据中的某些关键信息缺失或错误，可能影响过账分录的生成。例如，物料的评估类别、价格控制等设置不正确。 5. **公司代码和工厂设置问题**：公司代码和工厂的相关设置可能存在问题，导致系统无法确定正确的过账路径。例如，公司代码与工厂之间的关联配置不正确，或者工厂的评估区域设置与会计科目设置不匹配。 ### 解决办法 1. **检查科目设置** - 使用事务码 OBYC，进入自动过账配置界面。 - 找到与移动类型 303 相关的事务键（如 BSX、GBB 等），检查对应的总账科目是否正确设置。 - 如果发现科目设置不正确，进行相应的修改和保存。 2. **检查评估类设置** - 查看物料主数据中的评估类信息，确保其设置正确。可以使用事务码 MM03 查看物料主数据。 - 检查评估类与自动过账配置中的事务键是否匹配。如果不匹配，根据实际情况进行调整。 3. **检查移动类型配置** - 使用事务码 OMJJ，进入移动类型配置界面。 - 找到移动类型 303，检查其记账规则、评估类分配等设置是否正确。 - 如果发现配置有误，进行相应的修改和保存。 4. **检查物料主数据** - 使用事务码 MM03 查看物料主数据，确保物料的评估类别、价格控制等关键信息设置正确。 - 如果发现物料主数据存在错误，使用事务码 MM02 进行修改。 5. **检查公司代码和工厂设置** - 检查公司代码与工厂之间的关联配置，确保其正确无误。可以使用事务码 OX18 进行查看和修改。 - 检查工厂的评估区域设置，确保其与会计科目设置匹配。可以使用事务码 OMSY 进行查看和修改。 如果上述方法都无法解决问题，建议联系 SAP 系统管理员或顾问，进一步排查系统配置和数据问题。

entry.title: Re: SAP MM module
entry.content: 原因分析 1. **科目设置问题**：在 SAP 系统中，工厂间转移涉及到会计科目的记账。如果相关的自动过账配置没有正确设置，系统就无法确定对应的过账分录，从而提示过账分录不存在。例如，移动类型 303 对应的总账科目没有在 OBYC 事务码中正确维护。 2. **评估类设置问题**：评估类用于确定物料移动时的过账科目。如果物料主数据中的评估类设置不正确，或者评估类与自动过账配置不匹配，系统就无法找到合适的过账分录。 3. **移动类型配置问题**：移动类型 303 的配置可能存在错误或不完整。例如，移动类型的记账规则、评估类分配等设置不正确，会导致系统无法生成正确的过账分录。 4. **物料主数据问题**：物料主数据中的某些关键信息缺失或错误，可能影响过账分录的生成。例如，物料的评估类别、价格控制等设置不正确。 5. **公司代码和工厂设置问题**：公司代码和工厂的相关设置可能存在问题，导致系统无法确定正确的过账路径。例如，公司代码与工厂之间的关联配置不正确，或者工厂的评估区域设置与会计科目设置不匹配。 解决办法 1. **检查科目设置** - 使用事务码 OBYC，进入自动过账配置界面。 - 找到与移动类型 303 相关的事务键（如 BSX、GBB 等），检查对应的总账科目是否正确设置。 - 如果发现科目设置不正确，进行相应的修改和保存。 2. **检查评估类设置** - 查看物料主数据中的评估类信息，确保其设置正确。可以使用事务码 MM03 查看物料主数据。 - 检查评估类与自动过账配置中的事务键是否匹配。如果不匹配，根据实际情况进行调整。 3. **检查移动类型配置** - 使用事务码 OMJJ，进入移动类型配置界面。 - 找到移动类型 303，检查其记账规则、评估类分配等设置是否正确。 - 如果发现配置有误，进行相应的修改和保存。 4. **检查物料主数据** - 使用事务码 MM03 查看物料主数据，确保物料的评估类别、价格控制等关键信息设置正确。 - 如果发现物料主数据存在错误，使用事务码 MM02 进行修改。 5. **检查公司代码和工厂设置** - 检查公司代码与工厂之间的关联配置，确保其正确无误。可以使用事务码 OX18 进行查看和修改。 - 检查工厂的评估区域设置，确保其与会计科目设置匹配。可以使用事务码 OMSY 进行查看和修改。

entry.title: CAP project MDC table filterfield DATE search
entry.content:
Hello,
I apologize for my lack of proficiency in English.

I am currently working on implementing a feature in CAP development where the MDC Table uses a FilterField with odata.type.Date as the dataType to search dates using a DynamicDateRange.

Currently, range searches like BT (Between) work fine, but searches for specific dates, such as "EQ," "Yesterday," or "Tomorrow," are not working.

I am using an OData V4 query in the controller to get data and bind it to a JSONModel.
Additionally, I am using HANA DB, and the type of the cDate column is DateTime.
The data is stored in ISO format (e.g., 2025-01-22T12:27:06Z).
I have tried changing the type to DateTime in both the view and propertyInfo, but the results remain the same.

Here is the code attached.

{
            key: "cDate",
            label: "Date",
            visible: true,
            path: "cDate",
            dataType: "sap.ui.model.odata.type.Date",
            formatOptions : {
                pattern : "yyyy.MM.dd"
            }
        }

1. part of ProPertyInfo.js 
 <mdc:FilterField
                            id="dateFilter"
                            conditions="{$filters>/conditions/cDate}"
                            label="{i18n>date}"
                            propertyKey="cDate"
                            dataType="sap.ui.model.odata.type.Date"
                            delegate="{name: 'bulletinboardui/delegate/FieldBaseDelegate', payload: {}}"
                            maxConditions="1"
                            operators="EQ,BT,TODAYFROMTO,LASTDAYS,TODAY"
                            change="onPressDateFilter"
                            dataTypeFormatOptions="{pattern : 'yyyy.MM.dd'}"
                        />

2. Part of view.xml

I would appreciate your advice on resolving this issue.
Thank you

Let's say we need to create the shell from ECC on premise and need to create a shell on the cloud. Do we install an empty ECC EHP8 and then run a client copy via SCC8 with sap_cust? How about the ABAP customization? Does it copy the ABAP customization? Also, how about Z* tables? Will sap_cust create the tables without copying the data?

Or should we do a system copy from the on premise ECC to the cloud then do client copy? But this is very clumsy.

entry.title: Re: Change Sender E-Mail address for po approval notifica...
entry.content:
i use TC SWNCONFIG to send specific WF to a specific user u can do a lot of configoration AND change the sender.

if its aint working u need to make FB where u write a code to send email to a specific user and chosing the sender.

check this excample i did before:

 DATA: lt_mail         TYPE ptreq_email_receivers_tab.
  DATA: ls_mail         TYPE somlreci1.
  DATA  lv_recipient    TYPE adr6-smtp_addr.
  DATA: mailtext        TYPE string.
  DATA: ls_subject      TYPE so_obj_des.
  DATA: ls_body         TYPE soli.
  DATA: ls_starts       TYPE soli.
  DATA: datum           TYPE char10.
  DATA: lt_text         TYPE soli_tab.
  DATA: ls_text         TYPE soli.
  DATA: lo_sender       TYPE REF TO if_sender_bcs    VALUE IS INITIAL,
        lo_document     TYPE REF TO cl_document_bcs  VALUE IS INITIAL,
        lo_recipient    TYPE REF TO if_recipient_bcs VALUE IS INITIAL,
        lo_send_request TYPE REF TO cl_bcs           VALUE IS INITIAL.

  SELECT * FROM vbpa INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_vbpa)
   WHERE vbeln = @iv_vbeln.
  SORT lt_vbpa BY kunnr.

  LOOP AT lt_vbpa INTO DATA(ls_vbpa) WHERE
         parvw = 'Y0'.

    IF ls_vbpa-pernr <> '00000000'.

      SELECT SINGLE FROM pa0105
        FIELDS usrid_long
        WHERE pernr EQ @LS_vbpa-pernr
        AND   begda LE @SY-datum
        AND   endda GE @SY-datum
        AND   subty EQ '0010' INTO @LS_mail.


      CLEAR ls_vbpa.
    ENDIF.
  ENDLOOP.

  LOOP AT lt_vbpa INTO ls_vbpa WHERE
         parvw = 'ZZ'.
  ENDLOOP.

  SELECT SINGLE FROM kna1 AS a
    INNER JOIN t005t AS b ON a~land1 = b~land1
    FIELDS b~land1, a~name1
    WHERE a~kunnr = @LS_vbpa-kunnr AND
          b~spras = @SY-langu INTO @DATA(ls_kna1).

  ev_mail_its_pm = ls_mail.
  APPEND ls_mail TO lt_mail.


  WRITE iv_planstart TO datum DD/MM/YYYY.

  CONCATENATE 'Sie erhalten diese Email, weil Sie als Projektleiter*in für Digitalisierung zum Projekt<b>' ls_vbpa-vbeln  ls_kna1-name1  INTO ls_body SEPARATED BY space.
  CONCATENATE ' </b>in SAP hinterlegt sind. <b>Am ' datum '</b> findet der <b>Inbetriebnahme-Start</b> für genanntes Projekt statt. Bitte planen Sie zeitnah das <b>Digitalisierungs Kick-off Meeting</b> und alle ' INTO ls_starts SEPARATED BY space.

  ls_text = 'Sehr geehrte Kolleg*in,</br></br>'.
  APPEND ls_text TO lt_text.
  CLEAR: ls_text.
  APPEND ls_text TO lt_text.

  CLEAR: ls_text.
  ls_text = ls_body.
  APPEND ls_text TO lt_text.

  CLEAR:ls_text.
  ls_text = ls_starts.
  APPEND ls_text TO lt_text.

  CLEAR:ls_text.
  ls_text = 'weiteren Schritte zur Inbetriebnahme. <br> <br> Viel Erfolg im weiteren Projektverlauf! <br> Freundliche Grüße <br> Ihr Digitalisierungs-Team'.
  APPEND ls_text TO lt_text.



* create send request to handle mail sending
  TRY.
      lo_send_request = cl_bcs=>create_persistent( ).
    CATCH cx_send_req_bcs.
  ENDTRY.

  CONCATENATE 'Inbetriebnahme-Start' ls_vbpa-vbeln  ls_kna1-land1  INTO ls_subject SEPARATED BY space.

  TRY.
      lo_document = cl_document_bcs=>create_document(
                      i_type    = 'HTM'
                      i_text    = lt_text
                      i_subject = ls_subject
                       ).
    CATCH cx_document_bcs .
  ENDTRY.

* pass the document to send request
  TRY.
      lo_send_request->set_document( lo_document ).
    CATCH cx_send_req_bcs .
  ENDTRY.

** create sender object
*    TRY.
*        lo_sender = cl_cam_address_bcs=>create_internet_address( 'Rechnungen@krones.com' ).
*      CATCH cx_address_bcs.
*    ENDTRY.
** set sender
*    TRY.
*        lo_send_request->set_sender(
*          EXPORTING
*            i_sender = lo_sender ).
*      CATCH cx_send_req_bcs.
*    ENDTRY.

  lv_recipient = 'keine.ahnung@gmail.com'.
  LOOP AT lt_mail INTO lv_recipient.

* create recipient object
    TRY.
        lo_recipient = cl_cam_address_bcs=>create_internet_address(
                                            lv_recipient ).
      CATCH cx_address_bcs.
    ENDTRY.
    TRY.
        lo_send_request->add_recipient( i_recipient = lo_recipient ).
      CATCH cx_send_req_bcs.
    ENDTRY.
  ENDLOOP.

* set recipient
  TRY.
      lo_send_request->add_recipient(
        EXPORTING
          i_recipient = lo_recipient
          i_express = abap_false ).
    CATCH cx_send_req_bcs.
  ENDTRY.

* send email
  DATA: rv_mail_sent TYPE  boolean.
  TRY.
      lo_send_request->send(
        EXPORTING
         i_with_error_screen = abap_true
        RECEIVING
         result =  rv_mail_sent ).
    CATCH cx_send_req_bcs.
  ENDTRY.

* commit work to create mail transmition
  COMMIT WORK.

entry.title: SAP CPI to SAP S4 files missing while passing through SFTP
entry.content:
Hello All,

We have developed an iflow in CPI wherein we are picking files from SFTP server and posting it to the receiver SFTP SAP S4 path.

However, while processing few files, we found an issue that CPI is picking the file and sending to SAP S4 system but when we check in the AL11 path for the same file there is no trace of that file, because of this we are facing issue on random days. This issue is occurring on some random days not like every day we have this issue.

CPI is polling file every 5 min and, in each poll, it picks up 1 or 2 files max.

Is anyone facing same kind of issue or faced same issue, if there is any particular reason for this please let me know.

I checked this with SAP by raising ticket but couldn't get any proper solution.

Thanks,

Vinay

entry.title: Re: First steps to work with SAP Cloud ALM Deployment scenario for SAP ABAP systems (7.40 or higher)
entry.content:
Hi @DoloresCorrea , 

many thanks for this great blog post.

Is it by intention that the section "3.1.5.4. User to register the jobs" is missing/empty?

When i use my user to perform the setup the job for "Heartbeat" and "Scheduler" are created by my user. Is there something planned that these jobs will be "created by" the technical user which is setup for the background jobs? Can this lead to issues when the person who set up the connection left the company?

Looking forward to hear from you.

Best regards

Stephan

entry.title: Re: Master database creation failed for SAP ASE 16.0 SP03...
entry.content: Hello, This appears to be a known issue, described here: https://me.sap.com/notes/3018138 the workaround for situation when a new ASE server is built by using srvbuild/srvbuildres: - Move dataserver to be dataserver.bin. - Then create a text filenamed dataserver and put the full path to dataserver.bin -T11889 $@ into this text file. The parameter $@ passes arguments to the wrapper. - After that give the execute permission on the file dataserver. For example: 1. mv $SYBASE/ASE-16_0/bin/dataserver $SYBASE/ASE-16_0/bin/dataserver.bin 2. vi $SYBASE/ASE-16_0/bin/dataserver, input the following text to this file $SYBASE/ASE-16_0/bin/dataserver.bin -T11889 $@ 3. Give the execute permission on the file dataserver. chmod +x dataserver HTH

entry.title: SAP 分析云 2025 年第一季度新功能介绍 （二）
entry.content:
SAP 分析云是一款先进的商务分析云解决方案，集商业智能（BI）、增强分析、预测分析和企业规划功能于一体，消除单点解决方案和数据孤岛挑战，能提供您需要的全面云分析功能。

SAP 分析云 2025 年第一季度的新功能将于2月中下旬正式上线，本系列文章将简单为大家介绍本次更新的部分新功能。完整更新功能列表请查看SAP官方Help文档。

 

作业监控器框架

SAP分析云中，用户经常需要同时管理多个异步流程，例如数据操作、多步操作、数据导入导出以及智能预测等。这些流程在后台运行，用户则需要过一段时间去检查它们的运行状态。

为了优化用户体验，我们引入了一个集中的监控视图，让用户能够一目了然地监控所有正在运行的流程，并回看历史执行记录及其详细性能数据。这可以提高数据操作效率。另外，此功能还支持多个团队协同工作。

您可以在“系统”->“作业监控器”中访问此页面，或者在数据操作或多步操作的界面中点击右上角的按钮进入。

 

此作业监控器框架提供了一个基本的监控视图页面，里面展示了关键的技术信息，如功能名称、状态、持续时间和启动者等。用户点击任意流程，右侧会弹出其详细信息页面视图面板，用于深入查看具体内容。

 

您还可以通过左侧的筛选器来筛选和查看不同的操作流程。筛选器面板默认显示高级筛选器部分，时间范围预设为最近七天，作业类型默认为主要作业。

筛选器分为公用和自定义两种类型。公用筛选器可筛选时间、作业类型、名称、状态、总持续时间和启动者等。而自定义筛选器仅适用于数据操作这一作业类型，用户可以根据自己的需求选择特定的模型和目标版本进行筛选，如下图所示。

 

数据操作 - 简化跨模型复制步骤中的映射

在数据操作的自动映射过程中，系统关注货币和单位度量（unit measures）的准确性，以确保用户在进行货币和单位转换时能够避免错误并保持一致性。以下是更新的具体内容：

  • 货币度量映射规则更新：

系统只会将货币度量自动映射到具有相同货币单位的其他货币度量。这意味着，如果源货币和目标货币相同，系统会自动进行映射，否则不会自动映射。

  • 单位度量映射问题：

对于单位度量，在自动映射时系统不会对单位的一致性进行检查。例如，两个具有相同ID但不同单位的度量（如米和千米）可以实现自动映射。

  • 货币处理改进：

在处理货币时，系统不再检查它是一个主要结构账户模型还是度量模型。只有在主要结构度量模型中，系统会将货币复制到货币。对于主要结构账户模型，映射规则取决于源账户和目标账户。

 

 

 

数据操作 - 激活/取消激活数据操作步骤

为了进一步简化数据操作的开发过程，我们新增了一项功能：允许用户激活或取消激活单个数据操作步骤。此功能在多个场景下为用户提供了帮助：

用户现在可以将复杂的数据操作分解为更精细的步骤，并临时禁用某些步骤。这样可以评估它们对整体性能的影响，或者诊断一些常规的问题。例如，用户可以通过禁用一个步骤来比较两个不同性能优化步骤中哪个更为高效。

对于那些只有一个租户且无法在开发环境中测试新功能的用户来说，现在可以对尚未在生产环境中执行的步骤进行新版本的开发和测试。

一些客户可能需要对复杂的计算步骤进行归档或版本控制，这样以便将来能够直接引用历史计算结果或恢复至之前的状态。

打开数据操作的某个步骤后，您能够在右侧面板的右上方看到“激活”或“取消激活”步骤的按钮。取消激活步骤后，即使该步骤中的某些条目尚未填写完整或为空，也不会引发错误提示（例如，下图中展示的复制规则）。但激活此步骤后，未正确填写的条目会相应报错。

 

指南针

本次推出了新功能“指南针”，用户在SAC中可以使用指南针进行蒙特卡洛模拟，并且可以创建多个模拟场景进行比较，如果对结果满意，可以将其保存在文件存储库中，并与其他用户共享。使用蒙特卡洛模拟估算不确定业务事件的可能结果。这种强大的模拟方法有助于了解风险，并在面临不确定性时做出更明智的选择。

用户可以通过以下方式使用指南针：

  • 从指南针开始页面中直接创建模拟，或者通过故事中的表格创建模拟。
  • 在模拟中创建私有方案，作为回答业务问题的实验。
  • 在私有方案中，从可用动因中进行选择，然后通过设置动因的输入筛选器和随机性来配置动因。具体而言，可以在动因上设置值范围和概率分布类型，以定义其随机性。

 

 

  • 通过配置受限动因的特定筛选器从基础动因添加受限动因，并在受限动因上设置值范围和分布类型，以定义其随机性。

 

  • 之后在不同的精度模式下运行私有方案。

 

  • 根据模拟结果图，分析模拟结果的概率分布。还可以移动滑块，以了解实现低于或高于特定目标值的可能性。执行其他操作，例如定义案例设置、添加比较方案，可以深入了解模拟结果。

  

 

 

 

  • 保存后，可以通过发布私有方案来创建公用方案，使其可供有权限访问模拟的用户使用。

           

 

 

优化故事体验中新增 "onAfterDataEntryProcess" 表事件

本次更新中新增了 onAfterDataEntryProcess 表事件，用户在故事脚本中编写自定义计划流程，该事件在数据输入、复制粘贴或通过 submitData() API 更改表中的数据后触发，并且可以提供变化单元格的详细信息, 包括旧值和新值，以及上下文信息。一个常见的应用是使用这个功能完成用户输入数据的数值验证，即如果用户录入表格的数据不符合事件中的要求，则弹出警告或拒绝录入。

 

 

 

本文仅提供 SAP 分析云的最新功能和改进功能的高级概述。如果您对本博文的内容有任何疑问，可以在下方发表评论。如果您有具体的产品或技术问题，请在我们的中文 SAP Analytics Cloud 问答论坛上提问。访问我们的 SAP Analytics Cloud 社区页面，了解更多产品信息、卓越实践等。查看我们的 SAP 路线图浏览器，了解 SAP 分析云即将推出的更多功能。

entry.title: Settlement Documentation Revamped
entry.content:
Hi there!

Are you new to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and want to understand the settlement process in Overhead Accounting? Or perhaps you're an experienced user with specific questions? Either way, you're in the right place.

The settlement process in Overhead Accounting can be complex, but we've made it easier for you to understand by updating and restructuring the product assistance, which is available on the SAP Help Portal. Here, you can find a detailed overview of the process as well as important settings to consider.

Key Resources

- Settlement: This is your go-to resource for a detailed overview of the settlement process. It covers everything from basic concepts to advanced settings.

- Management Accounting and Margin Analysis: FAQ: Here, you'll find answers to the most commonly asked questions about Management Accounting and Margin Analysis, which are crucial to understand Overhead Accounting.

Both resources are based on the latest SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition release: 2502 (February 2025).

Why This Matters

A vast amount of information is available on different platforms and finding the right answers can be challenging. Our updated documentation aims to streamline this process by providing you with clear and concise information all in one place.

Want to get further details?

Refer to Overhead Accounting.

Do you have questions or comments?

Don’t hesitate to post them in the comments section. We’d love to hear from you and help you further.

entry.title: Re: Why do I still report an error after using a dynamic DELETE statement?
entry.content:
REPORT zzz_test.

DATA: ct_tab TYPE TABLE OF tadir,
      ldref  TYPE REF TO data.

FIELD-SYMBOLS: <lt_tab> TYPE STANDARD TABLE.


SELECT * FROM tadir INTO TABLE ct_tab UP TO 10 ROWS.

APPEND INITIAL LINE TO ct_tab.
APPEND INITIAL LINE TO ct_tab.
APPEND INITIAL LINE TO ct_tab.


CREATE DATA ldref LIKE ct_tab.

ASSIGN ldref->* TO <lt_tab>.

LOOP AT ct_tab ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_any>).
  IF <fs_any> IS NOT INITIAL.
    APPEND <fs_any> TO <lt_tab>.
  ENDIF.
ENDLOOP.


BREAK-POINT.

The result is in <lt_tab>.

entry.title: Highlights of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 Release
entry.content:
Welcome to a new era of innovation and seamless integration with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502! As we usher in 2025, it's the perfect time to celebrate the incredible journey we've shared with our public Cloud ERP community. Over the years, our customers have quickly gained extensive upgrade experience, all thanks to our continuous and seamless feature delivery. Reflecting on this progress, it's astonishing to note that our 33 major SaaS ERP release upgrades are equivalent to encapsulating 130+ years of upgrade experience based on the traditional on-premise deployment models. This showcases the high standard of our SaaS model. With the clean core approach and full lifecycle compliance embedded, we enable customers to harness the latest features instantly.

As our technological landscape continues to evolve, SAP remains at the forefront, driving innovations that empower businesses. I am excited to walk you through my key highlights of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 release.

Get ready for an exciting array of updates, including powerful AI enhancements, expanded capabilities of our generative AI co-pilot, Joule, and groundbreaking ERP innovations. Our 2502 release is here to further automate your operations, underpin agility in a cloud-first environment and drive more of your processes towards zero touch. Stay tuned as we dive into these transformative developments.

If you prefer watching rather than reading, you are welcome to check out my product update video: 

Figure 1: Check out my top highlights of the release in this video. 

 

AI-Powered UX Enhancements: Transforming User Interaction with Joule

Joule, our generative AI co-pilot, revolutionizes user experience by streamlining tasks through instant insights and intuitive navigation via natural language input. One notable feature is the new personalized card creation, enabling users to swiftly access vital ERP data such as purchase orders and delivery updates, thus, enhancing decision-making processes.

Figure 2: Get instant insights and swift access to your data with our natural language, generative AI copilot, Joule.

 

 

Our AI-driven UX innovations, AI-assisted smart summarization and AI-assisted easy filter, initially released as beta shipments in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408, have now become generally available. Smart summarization leverages artificial intelligence to condense complex data into easily understandable summaries, saving time and boosting efficiency. Easy filter allows users to apply detailed filters using natural language, simplifying data exploration.

Figure 3: AI-assisted smart summarization saves time and boosts efficiency by condensing complex data into easily understandable summaries.

 

 

Figure 4: AI-assisted easy filter simplifies data exploration and reduces effort.

 

 

As we progress with AI-assisted UX features, the upcoming SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 release introduces remarkable advancements in beta testing. These include: 

  • AI-assisted easy enterprise search,
  • AI-assisted smart personalization of My Home,  
  • and AI-assisted error explanation.  

Sign up through this link to start testing.

 

Enhancing Carbon Accountability with SAP Green Ledger

SAP Green Ledger is a transformative financial solution at the heart of carbon accounting. This is designed to integrate seamlessly with connected systems such as ERP and sustainability. By extending the scope of financial accounting to encompass carbon, it applies the principles of double-entry accounting to carbon quantities. These are posted on dimensions relevant to business steering, like profit centers and segments, offering a tangible perspective on carbon impact from provisioning through to delivery. The value proposition of SAP Green Ledger lies in its ability to track, value, and report the carbon footprint of end-to-end operations, with an emphasis on producing auditable and trustworthy reports. By analyzing carbon data alongside financial attributes, it generates insights pivotal for informed decision-making.

 

Figure 5: SAP Green Ledger integrates seamlessly with connected systems such as ERP and sustainability.

 

 

The key capabilities include integration support with other systems, replication of financial master data, importation and enrichment of material movements with carbon information and exporting carbon data for group reporting. Through its robust allocation management and comprehensive visualization, SAP Green Ledger allows businesses to compare carbon data directly with financial data, enhancing strategic understanding and operational transparency.

 

Enhancing Quality Management with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502: Expanding the “Manage Certificate Receipts” App with Create Mode

The SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 release introduces the create mode in the “Manage Certificate Receipts” app, building on the foundation of innovations introduced in the 2408 release. The original app allowed users to manage and link quality certificates to purchasing documents and goods receipts which improves compliance and traceability. The new Create Mode adds flexibility by enabling users to record certificates received before goods arrive, e.g., by email, and link them to material records upon arrival.

Figure 6: Record certificate receipts before goods arrival, ensuring smoother inventory processes.

 

This enhancement also improves traceability by providing access to existing certificates tied to purchasing documents and items, ensuring complete visibility across processes. Reduce potential error sources and strengthen both transparency and compliance processes with the further evolution of our certificate management capabilities. This enhancement solidifies the Manage Certificate Receipts App as a vital tool for higher efficiency.

 

Unlocking Transformation with GROW with SAP on AWS: A New Era in Cloud ERP

Having your Experience evolution on mind, I´d like to point you to GROW with SAP on AWS, an initiative that seamlessly integrates SAP's undeniable ERP expertise with AWS's expansive cloud infrastructure. With this, SAP is offering transformative solutions for organizations adopting SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. This alliance is a milestone in delivering extraordinary value, tailored for a diverse array of industries, scalability, security, and innovation. GROW with SAP on AWS features a modular design with prebuilt, industry-specific capabilities which allows businesses to scale effortlessly. Rapid deployment is facilitated by proven methodologies such as SAP Activate. Businesses can transition to even swifter deployments.

Figure 7: GROW with SAP on AWS.

 

 

The collaboration on AWS Marketplace simplifies procurement and accelerates adoption, empowering companies to thrive with agility and resilience in today's digital economy. GROW with SAP on AWS emerges beyond ERP, as a transformative enabler that drives the digital transformation frontier forward.

 

Expanding Opportunities with Scalable Solutions: Empowering Partners Through ABAP Cloud Add-Ons

With the general availability of scalable solutions on stack, partners are now equipped to build and extend solutions for their customers, creating significant value and expanding capabilities. This empowerment enables partners to develop, package, and broadly distribute their solutions as ABAP Cloud add-ons, reaching a larger customer base effectively.  Partners begin by developing their extensions within their own landscape and under their own namespace. After thorough testing, the solution is registered and assembled into an installable package. Once published by the partner, it becomes available to customers on the SAP Store. Customers can explore all their purchased and available products using the Discover Product app and can subsequently deploy these solutions in their three-system landscape.

Figure 8: Expand opportunities with scalable solutions.

 

 

This approach greatly complements the existing side-by-side extensibility concept to scale applications according to growing demand, ensuring they stay agile and responsive to rapid market changes. Partners can leverage these capabilities to innovate and differentiate themselves in the competitive market, aligning their offerings closely with customer needs and business requirements as an additional option.

For more information click here. 

 

Advancing Project Efficiency: Innovations in SAP Activate Release

In the ongoing evolution of SAP Activate, we are committed to enhancing our implementation and operational guidance, continuously leveraging insights from customer projects and, in this case, early adopters of Project Lines. Our latest release introduces refined guidance in project management, which enables teams to streamline their workflows and optimize resource allocation. We have also introduced improvements in receiving provisioned environments, making it easier and faster for customers to get started with their projects. Additionally, we have enhanced the identification of activities related to the GROW Adoption Plan, ensuring that users can seamlessly integrate these into their strategic roadmaps.

Another major advancement I’d like to mention is the integration of the “SAP Activate road map viewer” into the “SAP for Me” platform.

Figure 9: SAP Activate Road Map view.

 

 

This transition simplifies access for all users of the comprehensive methodology content of SAP Activate. Seamless learning and efficient project planning are important ingrediencies of a transformed demand with 5 generations of users.

 

Key Summary

In a nutshell, The SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 release introduces a balanced mix of infrastructure and experience investments, enhanced AI-driven user experiences, embedded AI features, and new or enhanced ERP capabilities, rounded off by the innovative SAP Green Ledger for integrated carbon accounting within financial systems. Additionally, ABAP Cloud Add-Ons give partners an additional tool at hands to extend and differentiate solutions effectively.


Another release that paves the way for businesses to optimize efficiency, scalability, and sustainability in their transformed ERP operations. As all previous releases, it is our invitation to you to join us in the ERP revolution and start your transformative journey. Keep exploring us and keep challenging us to unlock your company's full potential in the evolving digital landscape. Stay tuned for further details in the individual blog posts diving deeper into selected lines of business and industries.

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts. 

Feel free to follow me at BeSchulze and on LinkedIn to not miss any updates. 

Yours, Bert

 

📽️ Watch the Replays of Our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 Early Release Series!   

Between January 21 and January 24, we conducted a series of 21 engaging live sessions to showcase the thrilling innovations included in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 release. If you missed the live sessions, don't worry! You can still access the whole series or select the sessions that interest you the most. Seize this opportunity to watch them on demand at your convenience–anywhere, anytime.  

  

🔎Find All of Our Assets   

SAP S/4HANA Cloud is the foundation of the intelligent, sustainable enterprise and is an innovative, robust, and scalable ERP. We at Cloud ERP Product Success and Cloud Co-Innovation offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

entry.title: Public Cloud TM: prevent freight unit creation for PO (e.g. based on shipping condition)
entry.content:
Hello,

the freight unit creation for purchase orders (POs) considers the following document data: purchasing org, purchasing group, document type.

Unfortunately, these settings are too high-level for our business requirements: we'd prefer to include incoterms and/or at least the shipping condition as criteria for freight unit creation yes/no.

With the "Freight unit building rule condition", it is possible to use additional criteria (e.g. shipping condition) to determine the FU building rule, however, obviously, one cannot control whether the FU is created at all.

This seems to be meant with "If you have not specified an FUB rule, the system uses standard settings." in the SAP help: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/61e246f4b34c4e1790d8b7651c0b40a8/ffe2c7b83a494e88b0b76d6ab122a4fb.html?locale=en-US&version=2502.500

In S/4 private cloud, one can use BADIs "BADI_LO_TM_PUR/...SHP/...SLS" to influence the result of the determination of the logistic integration profile - which can also be used to prevent FU creation (by removing the determined profile). However, this BADI is not available in public cloud.

Any idea on how to prevent the FU creation for POs in public cloud?

Thanks & regards

Wolfgang

entry.title: Re: Use different exchange rates between B/S and P&L
entry.content:
Hi,

With regards to currency translation to a group presentation currency in accounting, we recommend the following:

  • Accounting
    • have an alternative measure in accounting capturing the group presentation currency (e.g. global currency)
    • Currency translation in accounting happens while posting. 
    • The closing rate cannot be available at that point in time
    • The translation in accounting allows you to have an accurate translation at the spot rate of the transaction for P&L and BS movements, which is the closest possible to get to accounting standards like IFRS. It also allows you to get a preliminary view on your group currency amounts at any time reflecting the latest accounting journals
  • Group Reporting
    • Do closing rate translation at closing rate in Group Reporting as part of your closing activities
    • integrate the original accounting group currency together with the local (functional) currency (e.g. release task)
    • run currency translation task task in GR
    • Result is that you have 2 group currency amounts. The original one translated at the spot rate of the transaction which is in line with the one in accounting, and the closing rate (e.g. for BS only).
    • A difference is calculated and stored on a separate translation key as well as on a dedicated subitem (transaction type) or in the case of equity on a separate FS item (currency translation reserve).

Hope this helps.

Kind regards,

Florian

entry.title: Re: Canada ECP- Cross-year retro payroll and tax for Cana...
entry.content:
Hello Woody,

Thank you for reviewing the scenario and providing your inputs. Standard calculation for Canada payroll has an option to bring forward the differences from prior year but the earnings are treated as non-periodic income and negative values are not taxed. Even if I change to regular taxable income and add to current pay period income (PP01/2025) to calculate the correct taxes, because of the 2 weeks lag for absences, next period (PP02/2025) will retro into PP01/2025 and reduce the income again if employee is on an unpaid absence. The taxable income for PP01/2025 goes negative and system does not calculate tax on negative income. So the refund of taxes is only for the original amount that was calculated and pay statement does not reflect the correct tax when compared to the earnings. Hope this explains the scenario in detail.

Thanks again and appreciate the inputs!

entry.title: Fetch user details (country) from IAS in the approuter
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community, 

I am working on a Cloud Foundry Approuter in SAP BTP and have the following custom middleware code:

 

const approuter = require('@sap/approuter');

const ar = approuter();

ar.beforeRequestHandler.use('/', function (req, res, next) {
    if (req.url.includes('/sap/opu/odata/MY_SERVICE/')) {
        req.headers.email_user = req.user.name;
        console.log("username");
        console.log(req.user.name);
        console.log("headers");
        console.log(req.headers);
        console.log("req");
        console.log(req);
    }
    
    next();
});

ar.start();

 

 

In this setup, I am trying to retrieve the country of the authenticated user. I know the user's email is accessible via req.user.name and I have also found the JWT Access Token in the request. However, the token does not include any region or country information.

Since country information is maintained in SAP IAS (Identity Authentication Service), I’m wondering:

  • Is it possible to retrieve the country of the user from the req object or its associated JWT token?
  • If not, what would be the recommended approach to fetch country information from IAS in an SAP BTP App Router application?

Any pointers or advice would be greatly appreciated!

Thanks in advance!
Mohammed

entry.title: Capturing Delta Requirements: A Guide to Fit-to-Standard Workshops
entry.content:
You are about to transform your business by replacing or upgrading your 15- or 20-year-old Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) platform. With a 'Cloud First' approach, you have presented the project's key objectives to management and chosen the SAP Business Suite as your future ERP. Your sandbox environment is set up with the Enterprise Management Layer and best practices. Now, you are ready for transformation. What's next?

Fit-to-Standard workshops

As per the SAP Activate methodology, a few tasks happened during the explore phase.

  1. Define business architecture <blog>
  2. Perform a fit-to-standard analysis to capture Delta requirements (backlog) using SAP Signavio Process Manager 

Points to note for process design during Fit-to-standard workshops

  • Workshops are driven by business process owners who are transformation champions and complimented by strong IT SMEs
    • This is an opportunity for the IT team to understand what outcome the business wants to achieve
  • Consider all relevant applications and steps to complete a business process
    • Don’t just focus on SAP-relevant steps. Include other system steps/interfaces, and don’t forget to capture manual steps
  • Create a structure of ‘Business Process Hierarchy’ before you begin workshops <blog>
    • The hierarchy will help you schedule workshops and assign subject matter experts
    • Hierarchy will help you to summarize/aggregate the findings at various levels

Capturing Delta Requirements in Fit-to-Standard Workshops

Fit-to-standard workshops are structured around Business Capabilities (focused on business or functional areas) or End-to-End Business Processes (focused on processes). These workshops explore functionality, demonstrate how solutions and best practices meet business requirements, and enable business process experts to define ‘to-be’ processes.

Delta requirements are identified, discussed, and then added to the backlog. The backlog consists of documented requirements, improvements, or functionalities required to complete a process. These items are prioritized, iteratively reviewed, and refined.

Understanding Business Process

Once you have defined the Business Process Hierarchy, you have a list of business activities.

Anatomy of a Business Activity (modular business process):

  • is a granular business process that creates value for internal or external stakeholders.
  • will have consistent naming conventions
    • will have a unique process flow that is defined using a ‘BPMN’ diagram
    • will have a clear start and end
    • will have a unique sequence of a task (level 5) to be performed. Each task is divided into steps (level 6)
    • Tasks (L5) will be performed by a role/agent. They will utilize application/s where data is used/analyzed and output/value is created. Policies may exist, and the organization may control business rules.
    • Each task (L5) will be broken down into steps (L6). These steps are performed in a given application or used by an interface. Steps will include required business logic/rules.

Understanding requirement collection

SAP provides standard solution flow diagrams and best practice diagrams. During the fit-to-standard workshop, delta requirements were collected at multiple levels. The following diagram shows the level at which the delta reequipments are captured.

Figure 1 - Capturing Detla requirements (backlog)

Process focused

1. Confirm whether a process is required at a process level.

This may require a scope discussion with the project team

  • Examples:
    • Create a purchase order for services > Required
    • Creating sales orders for services > Not required

2. What other process variants exist

Caution: This is a slippery slope for future enhancements. More variants will result in more business logic and business roles, especially when different business units require variances to execute the same processes

  • Examples:
    • Create a purchase order for the project purchase
    • Create a purchase order for the stock purchase
    • Create purchase orders for services based on contract

Application focused

3. Business rules: (expression of policy)

  • Examples:
    • Purchasing Approvals above a certain threshold
    • Applying tax codes and calculation

4. Business logic (expected system behaviours):

  • Examples:
    • Status updates for an outline agreement
    • Budget control checks

5. Enhancement/extension requirement

  • Examples:
    • A field on the PO is required to indicate ‘recycled material.’
    • Derive material handling requirements for dangerous goods
  • If business rules and business logic can not be implemented by application configuration, then additional extensions and enhancements are required.

Tip: Always ask clarifying questions while defining business logic/business rules to minimize enhancement/extension. Implementing legacy logic (or illogic)  without rationalizing will create technical debt.

6. Interface requirement

  • Example:
    • Send purchase orders to a specific vendor via an electronic interface

Data/Information focused

7. Data requirement

  • Example:
    • Enterprise Structure: Company codes, Plants, Sale Org
    • Master data: Material, Vendor, cost center etc.
    • Transaction data: Purchase orders, Purchase requisition
    • Various data requirements can be captured here, e.g. Data quality, data retention, data validation

8. Reporting requirement

  • Example:
    • Report to see purchases from a vendor in a given time (for ATIP)

9. Output/Forms

  • Example:
    • PO printouts (including PDF) as per Canada’s procurement guideline

User role/Security focused

10. Automation:

  • Example:
    • Auto approval for PO/IR less than a certain amount
  • Tip: Processes designed with clear business rules, business logic and data structure are prime for automation 

11. Security/Access control

  • Examples:
    • The purchasing officer can not enter goods receipt or invoice receipt
    • Mask credit card numbers

Rules of engagement during Fit-to-Standard (F2S) exercise.

  • This exercise simplifies and streamlines processes by minimizing variances, establishing clear business rules and logic, and documenting precise requirements to achieve desired outcomes. Avoid documenting requirements as ‘as-is’. The status quo is not your friend.
  • Convoluted and contradictory rules/logic will result in messy processes with unnecessary coding that must be maintained for a long time.
  • Simplify processes and data structures. This will positively impact user experience and reduce your application management costs for years.
  • Note that specific enhancements and integrations are inevitable if they must meet legislative requirements or interface with other existing systems. Focus on re/designing these extensions in a compliant manner
  • Once these requirements are reviewed, refined, and approved, SAP SMEs can work on developing process design documents and creating specs for configuration, enhancements, extension, and integration. Additional requirements, such as data conversion and non-functional requirements, can be captured at a higher level.
  • Use SAP Signavio Process Manager attributes to capture requirements and notes. Define requirement categorization, priorities, dependencies, etc. Capture dependencies
  • Don’t squander this is an opportunity to get close to the user; consider this as a ‘design thinking’ exercise.

Conclusion:

While consultants and the project team will eventually move on to other projects, the users will continue to execute these processes for the foreseeable future. It's crucial to design and implement the best processes possible, as users are relying upon you to enhance their efficiency and effectiveness. Your thoughtful design will have a lasting impact on their daily operations

Users are counting on you to bring out their best!

entry.title: Chart Display Issues with SAP BW Live Queries in SAC
entry.content:
Dear Community,

We are encountering an issue while using an SAP BW Live Query in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) during our migration process from Lumira Designer to SAC.
Our goal is to reuse the same BW queries in SAC that were previously implemented in Lumira Designer. However, we are facing problems with the display of a chart widget.

Here’s the situation:

  • The result in Lumira Designer (and also in Analysis for Office) displays correctly, as expected by our customer:

Lumira:

 Analysis for Office

 

 The query behind this result is quite complex and involves cell definitions. Despite this complexity, both Lumira Designer and AFO manage to display the charts as required.

In SAC, however, the chart displays incorrectly:

While I can manually adjust the colors to group the measures so they visually align with Lumira Designer and AFO, the legend remains incorrect:

 

  • The query output consists of filtered measures (5 measures = 5 columns) in the rows.
  • The columns contain a complex structure, including time selections and version information (e.g., ACT P011/2024).

 Since the query is highly complex, adapting or simplifying it is not an option.

My key question is:
Are there known limitations or workarounds in SAC for handling complex BW queries with such structures?

Any guidance or insights would be greatly appreciated!

Thank you in advance for your support.

Best regards,
Mario

entry.title: Celebrating the Latest Public Cloud Release for Product Compliance
entry.content:
Today marks an exciting milestone with the latest Public Cloud release for Product Compliance. This update brings new features, updated content, and improved processes, all designed to enhance user experience and streamline compliance management.

For those dealing with dangerous goods, staying compliant is crucial. The latest release includes new compliance requirement versions for the Australian Dangerous Goods Code (ADG) and the International Air Transport Association Dangerous Goods Regulations (IATA-DGR). For detailed instructions on connecting to our Regulatory Content Service, see the SAP Note 2999140. Another note on dangerous goods content, there is also a help page dedicated to all things content here.  A side note about the content service, it can be set up to deliver new compliance requirement versions, but you still need to activate the new version for it to be used in the system.

Packing instructions according to dangerous goods regulations data is also visible in the View Regulatory Data - Dangerous Goods app. Packing instructions according to dangerous goods regulation data shows the acceptable single and combination packagings. For combination packagings, the acceptable inner packagings, outer packagings and when applicable, the maximum permitted quantity in each inner or outer packaging are shown per packing group. This information is also part of the Dangerous Goods List for Packing Instructions.

  • For ADR, packing instructions for the following types of packages have been added:
    • Packages other than IBCs: P001, P002, P003, P004, P004, P005, P006, P099, P206, P207, P401, P402, P403, P404, P410, P502, P503, P907, P908, P909, P911
    • Intermediate bulk containers: IBC520, IBC620, IBC99
    • Large Packaging: LP02, LP03, LP101, LP102, LP200, LP 621, LP622, LP902, LP903, LP904, LP905, LP906, LP99

 

Here you can see the View Regulatory Data – Dangerous Goods app, and now there is a clickable link within the Enclosure Instruction Code. This link takes you to additional packaging information, structured like the actual dangerous goods regulations.

This is regulation dependent, and while this is not available for regulations, it is something we will continue to expand as data becomes available.

Moving on to product marketability, there are a lot of updated compliance requirement versions, and you can see complete list with additional information here. You can also see a complete list of available compliance requirement for product marketability here.

  • AU AIIC Status (Jul 2024)
  • CA DSL+NDSL Status (Jun 2024)
  • CA Drug Precursors (Jun 2024)
  • CH Chemical Risk Reduction Ordinance Status (Jun 2024)
  • CN RoHS (Jun 2024)
  • CN IECSC Status (May 2024)
  • EU Drug Precursors (May 2024)
  • EU ODS Status (Feb 2024)
  • EU REACH SVHC Status (Jun 2024)
  • EU Drug Precursors (May 2024)
  • EU REACH Annex XVII Status (May 2024)
  • JP ENCS Status (Jun 2024)
  • JP PDSCL Status (May 2024)
  • JP ISHA Status (Mar 2024 Rev.1)
  • JP CSCL Class 1 and 2 Status (Jul 2024)
  • JP CSCL Exempt Status (Jun 2024)
  • JP CSCL General Chemicals (Jul 2024)
  • MY Poisons List (May 2024)
  • NL ZZS List Status (May 2024)
  • RS Banned + Restricted Substances Status (Apr 2024)
  • SG Drug Precursors (May 2024)
  • US TSCA Export Notification (Jun 2024)
  • US TSCA Inventory Status (May 2024)
  • US TSCA SNUR Status (Jun 2024)
  • World IEC 62474 DSL (Jan 2024)
  • World PFAS Screening List Status (Apr 2024)

For safety data sheet management, authoring and occupational exposure limits there are 2 new compliance requirements and 14 new versions. The complete list with additional info can be found here. You can also see a complete list of available compliance requirement for SDS Management here,  SDS Authoring here, and Occupational Exposure Limits here. 

New compliance requirements

  • EU Specific Aerosol Labelling (Nov 2016)
  • EU Waste Code (Dec 2014)

New Versions

  • Bulgaria. Occupational Exposure Limits (Apr 2024)
  • Canada. Occupational Exposure Limits - British Columbia (Apr 2024)
  • China. Occupational Exposure Limits (Jul 2024)
  • Estonia. Occupational Exposure Limits (Apr 2024)
  • Germany. TRGS Occupational Exposure limits (Jun 2024)
  • Korea. Permissible Exposure Levels (Jun 2024)
  • Latvia. Occupational Exposure Limits (Apr 2024)
  • Netherlands. Occupational Exposure Limits (May 2024)
  • Poland. Occupational Exposure Limits (Jul 2024)
  • Romania. Occupational Exposure Limits (Mar 2024)
  • Singapore. Occupational Exposure Limits (May 2024)
  • Slovakia. Occupational Exposure Limits (Jun 2024)
  • Slovenia. Occupational Exposure Limits (Apr 2024)
  • USA. Workplace Environmental Exposure Levels (WEEL) (Jun 2024)

Finally, don't miss out on the SAP EHS and SAP Product Compliance & Stewardship communities in-person in Budapest, where we’ll be exploring the latest SAP S/4HANA innovations. Whether you are planning for future ERP projects, interested in a training opportunity for a member of your team,  or are looking to learn directly from other users, there won’t be another opportunity in 2025 to meet the full SAP ecosystem face-to-face. Click on the banner for more information.

entry.title: Re: How to post GI on the RF without TM?
entry.content:
Hi @VictorVillarpando ,

it is not possible to post goods issue on RF in Warehouse Management. RF is built for the persona of the Warehouse Operative who performs shopfloor tasks such as picking and packing. The goods issue functionality is available for the Warehouse Clerk, e.g. with the apps:

  • Manage Outbound Delivery Orders
  • Warehouse Monitor
  • Schedule Goods Issue Posting

If you require the function on a mobile RF device you have the options to:

  1. Build a custom app based on the warehousing APIs such as the Warehouse Outbound Delivery Order to post goods issue
  2. Extend the existing RF transactions with Screen Personas as described in this blog, e.g.by adding a new button to an existing screen which will call the API to post goods issue.

Best regards,
Christian

entry.title: Invalid Analytics Model - No mapping attribute found for dimension
entry.content:
Invalid Analytics Model - No mapping attribute found for dimension

This issue is done by the last SAP Datasphere update (09/01/2025 - 22/01/2025) - Note 3560510

1. It is important to have an unchanged copy available or (in production for example) because it is necessary to be able to find the technical dimensions and names used before the update

2. Identify associations with problems, Error messages in the analytical model direct you to the problematic dimensions

Here it is the Time-dependent Equipment dimension which comes from the Time-independent Equipment dimension

The entire branch needs to be redone
3. The original branch must be deleted, in order to be able to rename the new branch. So it's important to have another model available as a reference.
4. Select the Node N-1 of the problem node and you will find in the list of fields the available dimensions (associations)

 

The problematic dimension should be there, but not checked

 

5. Check the dimension and click the arrow on the right to go in. Here, tick 'Use associated dimension'

 

6. Go back a step and change the technical name of the dimension to the original.
This name is also the name of the association in view n-1

 

Technical name adapted in the analytical model:

The technical name of the association in the Equipment (Dimension) view, the original dimension of the problematic dimension:

 

7. Click the pencil next to the attributes and choose 'None' for the technical name. Otherwise the technical names of the fields change back and the reports will no longer find them.

 

It's a major part because this technical names on attribut have an impact on your SAC report ! 

Be careful that if in the past you had defined a suffix or prefix, you must put the same one back (see your AM prod)

 

8. Now you can add the fields of the dimension. Also reference an unchanged template to check if the description has not been changed.

 

9. Repeat for the rest of the branch and for other problematic dimensions.

Note, in Production at the level of SAC reporting, we don't see the impact of the Datasphere update because the AM works on the basis of the deployed version but once you want to redeploy it it will be wrong.

By Jorg Vandersteen

entry.title: Error: 'Template variant cannot be deleted; it has been used to output a document'
entry.content:
When attempting to delete the form template, the system displays the following error message:

'Template variant cannot be deleted; as it has been used in an output document'


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Error: 'Template variant cannot be deleted; it has be...
entry.content:
As per the system's standard behavior, it is not possible to delete a form template once it has been used in an output document. Deleting form templates that are already used in business documents can lead to inconsistency.

Note: This applies to all form templates in the system.

entry.title: Re: How to create picking warehouse tasks for an outbound...
entry.content:
Hi @VictorVillarpando ,

it is not possible to create picking warehouse task on RF in Warehouse Management. RF is built for the persona of the Warehouse Operative who performs shopfloor tasks such as picking and packing. The picking warehouse task creation is available for the Warehouse Clerk, e.g. with the apps:

  • Manage Outbound Delivery Orders
  • Warehouse Monitor
  • Schedule Warehouse Task Creation

If you require the function on a mobile RF device you have the options to:

  1. Build a custom app based on the warehousing APIs such as the Warehouse Order and Task to create picking warehouse task
  2. Extend the existing RF transactions with Screen Personas as described in this blog, e.g.by adding a new button to an existing screen which will call the API to create picking warehouse tasks

entry.title: Re: Deactivation of Compliance Requirements in SAP S/4HAN...
entry.content:
Hi @HarryStraussReal, for your situation, yes you need to manually deactivate the old version because the old version needs to first be replaced by the new version in compliance assessments of the compliance view. This can be the case when both are still active because both are still valid. This can sometimes happen, so therefore in order to protect products in different scenarios; the situation is possible. Once the new version is active, all assessments are recalculated and then old version can then safely be deactivated without causing any issues. Here is a short bit from the Help Portal which I think provides additional information.

 

 
As a global product stewardship specialist, you can use the Activate Compliance Requirements – Safety Data Sheet app to:

  • Get a list of the activated compliance requirement versions so you have an overview of which compliance requirement versions are being used.

  • Set an active compliance requirement version to inactive so that it can’t be used anymore in your products.

  • Stay informed if an active compliance requirement version has been updated so you can decide if the relevant safety data sheet assessments have to be recalculated.

  • Get a list of the inactive compliance requirement versions so that you can decide if any of them need to be activated.

  • In case of compliance requirements using the Components with Occupational Exposure Limits or the Hazardous Ingredients compliance patterns, during activation, select your preferred legal area needed to determine the precise composition. The composition is used as base data for propagation, calculation, and substance selection. You can revise the customer-specific regulatory lists that are assigned to the requirement. You can also view the delivered regulatory list assignments to this requirement. Once you've carried out the configuration, you can activate the requirement for use in follow-on business processes.

  • Trigger the recalculation of Occupational Exposure Limits (OEL) assessments that were calculated for a specific compliance requirement version when the version has been changed or corrected.

What Happens with the Entered Data

You activate compliance requirement versions in the Activate Compliance Requirements – Safety Data Sheet app after the content provider or product stewardship specialist for product compliance has created and released them in the Manage Compliance Requirements - Safety Data Sheet app. The product stewardship specialist for product compliance can add active compliance requirements to compliance purposes in the Manage Compliance Purposes app, and activate new compliance purposes. The product stewardship specialist for product compliance can assign active compliance purposes to products, and the master data specialist for product compliance can assign compliance purposes to raw materials.

I hope this helps to clarify, we are working on improving the information in the Help Portal and automating the Content Service more and more to make the entire compliance requirement version activation and deactivation easier.

entry.title: Lowering the SFC quantity In SAP DM
entry.content:
I need to lower the sfc or build quantity, the desired result is the user presses a button on the pod, this changes sfc/build quantity to match the done qty, when the done quantity is hit on this or the next operation. A final confirmation of CNF is received in the ERP and the order is moved out of the pod. This is on a production order in a discrete environment.  

I was able to do this in MII by lowering the build quantity but can't find an api in DM to do this.

Thanks
